_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of how hubs, switches, routers work together in the design of a network.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data and AI organizational design patterns, including centralized CoE, federated, and hub-and-spoke models, and the conditions under which each is appropriate.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-K-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-K-013 (TF, cosine: 0.459), DQ-K-007 (DQ, cosine: 0.447).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.5/10 (raw 0.551). Shared vocabulary: [design, knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [hubs, network, routers, switches, together, work] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, appropriate, centralized, coe, conditions, data] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.884 (P=0.906, R=0.864). P exceeds R by 0.042 — the DCWF element's 14 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 23 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of how hubs, switches, routers work together in the design of a networ" and "Knowledge of data and AI organizational design patterns, including centralized C" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.492 < STS 0.551 (gap -0.059). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [hubs, network, routers] and [ai, appropriate, centralized] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.9%. Shared tokens: [design, knowledge]. DCWF-only: [hubs, network, routers, switches, together, work]. WIDAI-only: [ai, appropriate, centralized, coe, conditions, data].
